title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 4 - A Look at the Core Sets (Part Three)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0YdWcjA5acg

Daniel (@dungeondive) dives into the settlement phase of Shadows of Brimstone, examining what happens between missions in the core sets' frontier towns and feudal villages.

After each quest, heroes travel back to town—rolling for hazards along the way—where they can heal, shop, gamble, and encounter all manner of trouble. The base travel hazard chart is stingy (only 16 entries), so Daniel recommends supplementing it with fan-made content like the hex crawl expansion. Once in town, you visit locations: the frontier outpost, blacksmith, church, saloon, doc's office, and general store, each with its own random event chart and shopping options. The longer you linger, the more dangerous town events become—void twisters, plagues, even full-on overruns.

Daniel walks through both Western and Eastern settlements, noting they're more similar than he'd like. He's eagerly awaiting the Feudal Village expansion to give the Japanese setting more distinct character. A persistent gripe: town purchases don't come with loot cards, so you need character sheets to track them—or hope fan-made card sets survive Flying Frog's copyright takedowns. The Frontier Town expansion, covered next in the series, is flagged as essential for elevating this phase from pleasant diversion to genuine highlight.

----

Do you enjoy the downtime between dungeon runs, or would you rather get straight back into the action?
